refinement_sections.content: We want to find out if the average sales of our new product are higher than a specific number we have in mind. This will help us know if the product is doing well in the market.

We need to create two statements. One is the null hypothesis, which says that the average sales are not greater than the benchmark. The other is the alternative hypothesis, which says that the average sales are greater than the benchmark.
We will use sales data from the last three months. We need to make sure we have enough data points to make our test valid.
We will choose a hypothesis test. I think a t-test might be suitable since we are comparing the average of our sample to a known benchmark.
We should assume that the sales data is normally distributed. Also, we need to check if the sample size is big enough to apply the t-test.
Finally, we will write a report that includes our method, the results of the test, and what we think the company should do based on these results.
